name: video-processor
description: Process video files with audio extraction, format conversion (mp4, webm), and Whisper transcription. Use when user mentions video conversion, audio extraction, transcription, mp4, webm, ffmpeg, or whisper transcription.
capabilities: Extract audio from any video file to WAV, MP3, AAC, or FLAC
Convert videos to MP4 with customizable codecs and quality settings
Convert videos to WebM format optimized for web streaming
Transcribe audio/video using OpenAI Whisper with multiple model sizes
Generate subtitles in SRT, VTT, or JSON formats with timestamps
author: EGAdams
securitySummary: This is a legitimate video processing utility. All subprocess calls use list-based arguments (safe from injection). The static scanner flagged documentation syntax (backticks, pipes) and markdown tables as suspicious patterns. No actual crypto or C2 behavior exists. Input paths are validated via click.Path() and arguments are constrained by click.Choice() enums. Temporary files are properly cleaned up.
valueStatement: Process videos without manual command-line work. Extract audio, convert formats, and generate transcripts with simple commands. Perfect for content creators, educators, and developers.
userTitle: Convert and transcribe videos with FFmpeg and Whisper
limitations: Requires FFmpeg and OpenAI Whisper pre-installed on system
No cloud processing - all operations run locally on your machine
Large video files may require significant processing time and resources

useCases.title: Convert videos for web
useCases.description: Convert camera footage to web formats and generate subtitles

useCases.title: Create lecture transcripts
useCases.description: Extract audio from recordings and generate accurate transcripts

useCases.title: Process video assets
useCases.description: Convert videos to standardized formats for apps

promptTemplates.title: Quick video conversion
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert this video file to MP4 format: input_video.avi

promptTemplates.title: Extract and transcribe
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract the audio from meeting_recording.mp4 and transcribe it

promptTemplates.title: Web-ready with subtitles
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert presentation.mp4 to WebM and generate SRT subtitles

promptTemplates.title: High-quality transcription
promptTemplates.prompt: Transcribe this Spanish interview using medium Whisper model: entrevista.mp4

bestPractices: Use the base Whisper model for quick drafts and small model for production quality
Extract audio first for long videos, then transcribe in segments for better performance
Convert to WebM with VP9 codec for best web compression and streaming quality
antiPatterns: Do not process extremely large files without considering system resources
Avoid using the large Whisper model unless maximum accuracy is essential
Do not store sensitive information in video transcripts without encryption

faq.question: Which video formats are supported?
faq.answer: MP4, AVI, MOV, MKV, WebM, FLV, WMV for video input. Audio: WAV, MP3, AAC, FLAC, M4A, OGG.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything?
faq.answer: Yes, install FFmpeg and OpenAI Whisper on your system before using this skill.

faq.question: Can it handle long videos?
faq.answer: Yes, but consider extracting audio first for very long videos to avoid timeouts.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: All processing happens locally on your machine. No files are uploaded to external servers.

faq.question: Why is transcription slow?
faq.answer: Larger Whisper models need more processing time and memory for better accuracy.

faq.question: How accurate is transcription?
faq.answer: Accuracy depends on audio quality and model size. Use medium or large models for best results.
